---
title: "Model Loading"
subtitle: "Computer Graphics - Loading 3D Models with Assimp"
author: "Gustavo Reis"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dracula
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    preview-links: auto
    logo: "images/OpenGL/Opengl-logo.svg"
    css: custom.css
    footer: "Model Loading with Assimp"
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    highlight-style: github-dark
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
---

## Introduction

Until now, we've been creating simple geometric shapes (triangles, cubes) by manually defining vertices.

::: {.fragment}
**Real applications** need complex 3D models created by artists in modeling software like Blender, Maya, or 3ds Max.
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Problem**: These models are stored in various file formats (.obj, .fbx, .gltf, .dae...) with different structures.
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Solution**: Use **Assimp** (Open Asset Import Library) to load models in a unified way!
:::

---

# Model File Formats

## Common 3D Formats

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Simple Formats:**

- **OBJ**: Text-based, widely supported
- **STL**: Simple triangulated meshes
- **PLY**: Point clouds and meshes

**Advantages:**

- Easy to parse
- Human-readable (OBJ)
- Great for learning
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Complex Formats:**

- **FBX**: Autodesk proprietary
- **GLTF/GLB**: Modern, efficient
- **COLLADA (.dae)**: XML-based
- **Blend**: Blender native

**Features:**

- Animations
- Materials
- Textures
- Scene hierarchy
:::
:::

## OBJ Format Example

```obj
# Vertices
v 0.0 0.0 0.0
v 0.0 1.0 0.0
v 1.0 0.0 0.0
v 1.0 1.0 0.0

# Texture coordinates
vt 0.0 0.0
vt 0.0 1.0
vt 1.0 0.0
vt 1.0 1.0

# Normals
vn 0.0 0.0 1.0
vn 0.0 0.0 1.0

# Faces (vertex/texcoord/normal)
f 1/1/1 2/2/1 3/3/1
f 3/3/1 2/2/1 4/4/1
```

::: {.callout-note}
OBJ is simple but lacks animations and scene information. Good for static models!
:::

## GLTF - Modern Standard

::: {.callout-tip}
**GLTF** (GL Transmission Format) is the "JPEG of 3D" - efficient, standardized, widely supported.
:::

**Advantages:**

- Compact binary format (GLB)
- PBR materials built-in
- Animation support
- Scene hierarchy
- Efficient for real-time use
- Open standard by Khronos

**Use GLTF** for modern applications when possible!

---

# Introducing Assimp

## What is Assimp?

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
**Open Asset Import Library**

- Loads 40+ file formats
- Unified interface
- Post-processing options
- Cross-platform
- Active development
- Free and open-source (BSD license)
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](https://www.assimp.org/images/assimp_banner_512.png)
:::
:::

## Supported Formats

**Common formats Assimp supports:**

- Autodesk (.fbx, .3ds, .dxf)
- Wavefront (.obj)
- Collada (.dae)
- GLTF (.gltf, .glb)
- Blender (.blend)
- Stereolithography (.stl)
- DirectX (.x)
- And many more...

::: {.callout-note}
Check [assimp.org](https://www.assimp.org/) for complete format list.
:::

## Installing Assimp

::: {.panel-tabset}
### CMake
```cmake
find_package(assimp REQUIRED)
target_link_libraries(your_app PRIVATE assimp::assimp)
```

### vcpkg
```bash
vcpkg install assimp
```

### Debian/Ubuntu
```bash
sudo apt-get install libassimp-dev
```

### Building from source
```bash
git clone https://github.com/assimp/assimp.git
cd assimp
cmake -B build
cmake --build build
cmake --install build
```
:::

## Basic Include

```cpp
#include <assimp/Importer.hpp>
#include <assimp/scene.h>
#include <assimp/postprocess.h>
```

::: {.incremental}
- **Importer.hpp**: Main importer class
- **scene.h**: Scene data structures
- **postprocess.h**: Post-processing flags
:::

---

# Assimp Data Structures

## The Scene Object

```cpp
const aiScene* scene = importer.ReadFile(path, flags);
```

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**aiScene contains:**

- Meshes array
- Materials array
- Animations
- Textures (embedded)
- Lights
- Cameras
- Scene graph (node hierarchy)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
```cpp
struct aiScene {
    aiMesh** mMeshes;
    unsigned int mNumMeshes;
    
    aiMaterial** mMaterials;
    unsigned int mNumMaterials;
    
    aiNode* mRootNode;
    
    aiAnimation** mAnimations;
    // ... more members
};
```
:::
:::

## aiMesh Structure

```cpp
struct aiMesh {
    aiVector3D* mVertices;      // Positions
    aiVector3D* mNormals;       // Normals
    aiVector3D* mTextureCoords[AI_MAX_NUMBER_OF_TEXTURECOORDS];
    aiVector3D* mTangents;
    aiVector3D* mBitangents;
    aiColor4D*  mColors[AI_MAX_NUMBER_OF_COLOR_SETS];
    
    aiFace* mFaces;             // Indices
    unsigned int mNumFaces;
    unsigned int mNumVertices;
    
    unsigned int mMaterialIndex;
};
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Each **aiMesh** represents a single drawable object with one material.
:::

## aiFace Structure

```cpp
struct aiFace {
    unsigned int* mIndices;
    unsigned int mNumIndices;
};
```

::: {.incremental}
- Typically triangles (3 indices)
- Assimp can triangulate for you
- Use post-processing flag: `aiProcess_Triangulate`
:::

## aiNode - Scene Graph

```cpp
struct aiNode {
    aiString mName;
    aiMatrix4x4 mTransformation;
    
    aiNode* mParent;
    aiNode** mChildren;
    unsigned int mNumChildren;
    
    unsigned int* mMeshes;  // Indices into scene->mMeshes
    unsigned int mNumMeshes;
};
```

::: {.fragment}
**Scene graph** allows hierarchical transformations (parent-child relationships).
:::

## aiMaterial

```cpp
struct aiMaterial {
    aiString GetTexture(aiTextureType type, unsigned int index);
    
    aiColor3D GetDiffuseColor();
    aiColor3D GetSpecularColor();
    float GetShininess();
    // ... many more properties
};
```

::: {.incremental}
- Contains material properties and texture paths
- Different properties depending on shading model
- Use `aiGetMaterialProperty` for custom properties
:::

---

# Our Mesh Class

## Mesh Class Design

```cpp
class Mesh {
public:
    struct Vertex {
        glm::vec3 Position;
        glm::vec3 Normal;
        glm::vec2 TexCoords;
        glm::vec3 Tangent;
        glm::vec3 Bitangent;
    };
    
    struct Texture {
        unsigned int id;
        std::string type;  // diffuse, specular, normal, height
        std::string path;
    };
    
    std::vector<Vertex> vertices;
    std::vector<unsigned int> indices;
    std::vector<Texture> textures;
    
    Mesh(std::vector<Vertex> vertices, 
         std::vector<unsigned int> indices,
         std::vector<Texture> textures);
    
    void Draw(const Shader& shader) const;
    
private:
    unsigned int VAO, VBO, EBO;
    void setupMesh();
};
```

## Mesh Constructor

```cpp
Mesh::Mesh(std::vector<Vertex> verts, 
           std::vector<unsigned int> inds,
           std::vector<Texture> texs)
    : vertices(std::move(verts))
    , indices(std::move(inds))
    , textures(std::move(texs))
{
    setupMesh();
}
```

::: {.callout-note}
Using `std::move` for efficiency - avoids copying large vectors.
:::

## setupMesh Implementation {.smaller}

```cpp
void Mesh::setupMesh() {
    glGenVertexArrays(1, &VAO);
    glGenBuffers(1, &VBO);
    glGenBuffers(1, &EBO);
    
    glBindVertexArray(VAO);
    
    glBindBuffer(GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, VBO);
    glBufferData(GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, vertices.size() * sizeof(Vertex),
                 vertices.data(), GL_STATIC_DRAW);
    
    glBindBuffer(GL_ELEMENT_ARRAY_BUFFER, EBO);
    glBufferData(GL_ELEMENT_ARRAY_BUFFER, indices.size() * sizeof(unsigned int),
                 indices.data(), GL_STATIC_DRAW);
    
    // Vertex positions
    glEnableVertexAttribArray(0);
    glVertexAttribPointer(0, 3, GL_FLOAT, GL_FALSE, sizeof(Vertex), 
                          (void*)offsetof(Vertex, Position));
    
    // Vertex normals
    glEnableVertexAttribArray(1);
    glVertexAttribPointer(1, 3, GL_FLOAT, GL_FALSE, sizeof(Vertex),
                          (void*)offsetof(Vertex, Normal));
    
    // Vertex texture coords
    glEnableVertexAttribArray(2);
    glVertexAttribPointer(2, 2, GL_FLOAT, GL_FALSE, sizeof(Vertex),
                          (void*)offsetof(Vertex, TexCoords));
    
    // Tangent
    glEnableVertexAttribArray(3);
    glVertexAttribPointer(3, 3, GL_FLOAT, GL_FALSE, sizeof(Vertex),
                          (void*)offsetof(Vertex, Tangent));
    
    // Bitangent
    glEnableVertexAttribArray(4);
    glVertexAttribPointer(4, 3, GL_FLOAT, GL_FALSE, sizeof(Vertex),
                          (void*)offsetof(Vertex, Bitangent));
    
    glBindVertexArray(0);
}
```

## Mesh::Draw Method {.smaller}

```cpp
void Mesh::Draw(const Shader& shader) const {
    // Bind textures
    unsigned int diffuseNr = 1;
    unsigned int specularNr = 1;
    unsigned int normalNr = 1;
    unsigned int heightNr = 1;
    
    for (size_t i = 0; i < textures.size(); ++i) {
        glActiveTexture(GL_TEXTURE0 + i);
        
        std::string number;
        std::string name = textures[i].type;
        
        if (name == "texture_diffuse")
            number = std::to_string(diffuseNr++);
        else if (name == "texture_specular")
            number = std::to_string(specularNr++);
        else if (name == "texture_normal")
            number = std::to_string(normalNr++);
        else if (name == "texture_height")
            number = std::to_string(heightNr++);
        
        shader.setInt(("material." + name + number).c_str(), i);
        glBindTexture(GL_TEXTURE_2D, textures[i].id);
    }
    
    // Draw mesh
    glBindVertexArray(VAO);
    glDrawElements(GL_TRIANGLES, indices.size(), GL_UNSIGNED_INT, 0);
    glBindVertexArray(0);
    
    glActiveTexture(GL_TEXTURE0);
}
```

---

# Model Class

## Model Class Interface

```cpp
class Model {
public:
    Model(const std::string& path) {
        loadModel(path);
    }
    
    void Draw(const Shader& shader) const {
        for (const auto& mesh : meshes)
            mesh.Draw(shader);
    }
    
private:
    std::vector<Mesh> meshes;
    std::string directory;
    std::vector<Mesh::Texture> textures_loaded;
    
    void loadModel(const std::string& path);
    void processNode(aiNode* node, const aiScene* scene);
    Mesh processMesh(aiMesh* mesh, const aiScene* scene);
    std::vector<Mesh::Texture> loadMaterialTextures(
        aiMaterial* mat, aiTextureType type, std::string typeName);
};
```

## Loading a Model

```cpp
void Model::loadModel(const std::string& path) {
    Assimp::Importer importer;
    const aiScene* scene = importer.ReadFile(path,
        aiProcess_Triangulate |
        aiProcess_FlipUVs |
        aiProcess_CalcTangentSpace |
        aiProcess_GenNormals
    );
    
    if (!scene || scene->mFlags & AI_SCENE_FLAGS_INCOMPLETE || !scene->mRootNode) {
        std::cerr << "ERROR::ASSIMP::" << importer.GetErrorString() << '\n';
        return;
    }
    
    directory = path.substr(0, path.find_last_of('/'));
    
    processNode(scene->mRootNode, scene);
}
```

## Post-Processing Flags

```cpp
aiProcess_Triangulate        // Convert to triangles
aiProcess_FlipUVs            // Flip texture coordinates Y axis
aiProcess_GenNormals         // Generate normals if missing
aiProcess_CalcTangentSpace   // Calculate tangent and bitangent
aiProcess_JoinIdenticalVertices  // Optimize: merge duplicate vertices
aiProcess_SortByPType        // Split by primitive type
aiProcess_OptimizeMeshes     // Reduce mesh count
aiProcess_OptimizeGraph      // Optimize scene hierarchy
```

::: {.callout-tip}
**Common combination:**
```cpp
aiProcess_Triangulate | aiProcess_FlipUVs | aiProcess_CalcTangentSpace
```
:::

## Processing Node Hierarchy

```cpp
void Model::processNode(aiNode* node, const aiScene* scene) {
    // Process all meshes in current node
    for (unsigned int i = 0; i < node->mNumMeshes; ++i) {
        aiMesh* mesh = scene->mMeshes[node->mMeshes[i]];
        meshes.push_back(processMesh(mesh, scene));
    }
    
    // Recursively process child nodes
    for (unsigned int i = 0; i < node->mNumChildren; ++i) {
        processNode(node->mChildren[i], scene);
    }
}
```

::: {.fragment}
**Recursion** traverses the entire scene graph!
:::

## Processing a Mesh {.smaller}

```cpp
Mesh Model::processMesh(aiMesh* mesh, const aiScene* scene) {
    std::vector<Mesh::Vertex> vertices;
    std::vector<unsigned int> indices;
    std::vector<Mesh::Texture> textures;
    
    // Process vertices
    for (unsigned int i = 0; i < mesh->mNumVertices; ++i) {
        Mesh::Vertex vertex;
        
        // Position
        vertex.Position = glm::vec3(
            mesh->mVertices[i].x,
            mesh->mVertices[i].y,
            mesh->mVertices[i].z
        );
        
        // Normal
        if (mesh->HasNormals()) {
            vertex.Normal = glm::vec3(
                mesh->mNormals[i].x,
                mesh->mNormals[i].y,
                mesh->mNormals[i].z
            );
        }
        
        // Texture coordinates
        if (mesh->mTextureCoords[0]) {
            vertex.TexCoords = glm::vec2(
                mesh->mTextureCoords[0][i].x,
                mesh->mTextureCoords[0][i].y
            );
            
            // Tangent
            vertex.Tangent = glm::vec3(
                mesh->mTangents[i].x,
                mesh->mTangents[i].y,
                mesh->mTangents[i].z
            );
            
            // Bitangent
            vertex.Bitangent = glm::vec3(
                mesh->mBitangents[i].x,
                mesh->mBitangents[i].y,
                mesh->mBitangents[i].z
            );
        } else {
            vertex.TexCoords = glm::vec2(0.0f, 0.0f);
        }
        
        vertices.push_back(vertex);
    }
    
    // Process indices
    for (unsigned int i = 0; i < mesh->mNumFaces; ++i) {
        aiFace face = mesh->mFaces[i];
        for (unsigned int j = 0; j < face.mNumIndices; ++j)
            indices.push_back(face.mIndices[j]);
    }
    
    // Process materials
    if (mesh->mMaterialIndex >= 0) {
        aiMaterial* material = scene->mMaterials[mesh->mMaterialIndex];
        
        // Diffuse maps
        std::vector<Mesh::Texture> diffuseMaps = loadMaterialTextures(
            material, aiTextureType_DIFFUSE, "texture_diffuse");
        textures.insert(textures.end(), diffuseMaps.begin(), diffuseMaps.end());
        
        // Specular maps
        std::vector<Mesh::Texture> specularMaps = loadMaterialTextures(
            material, aiTextureType_SPECULAR, "texture_specular");
        textures.insert(textures.end(), specularMaps.begin(), specularMaps.end());
        
        // Normal maps
        std::vector<Mesh::Texture> normalMaps = loadMaterialTextures(
            material, aiTextureType_NORMALS, "texture_normal");
        textures.insert(textures.end(), normalMaps.begin(), normalMaps.end());
        
        // Height maps
        std::vector<Mesh::Texture> heightMaps = loadMaterialTextures(
            material, aiTextureType_HEIGHT, "texture_height");
        textures.insert(textures.end(), heightMaps.begin(), heightMaps.end());
    }
    
    return Mesh(vertices, indices, textures);
}
```

## Loading Material Textures {.smaller}

```cpp
std::vector<Mesh::Texture> Model::loadMaterialTextures(
    aiMaterial* mat, aiTextureType type, std::string typeName)
{
    std::vector<Mesh::Texture> textures;
    
    for (unsigned int i = 0; i < mat->GetTextureCount(type); ++i) {
        aiString str;
        mat->GetTexture(type, i, &str);
        
        // Check if texture was already loaded
        bool skip = false;
        for (const auto& loadedTex : textures_loaded) {
            if (std::strcmp(loadedTex.path.data(), str.C_Str()) == 0) {
                textures.push_back(loadedTex);
                skip = true;
                break;
            }
        }
        
        if (!skip) {
            Mesh::Texture texture;
            texture.id = TextureFromFile(str.C_Str(), directory);
            texture.type = typeName;
            texture.path = str.C_Str();
            textures.push_back(texture);
            textures_loaded.push_back(texture);
        }
    }
    
    return textures;
}
```

::: {.callout-tip}
**Optimization**: We cache loaded textures to avoid loading the same texture multiple times!
:::

## TextureFromFile Helper

```cpp
unsigned int TextureFromFile(const char* path, const std::string& directory) {
    std::string filename = directory + '/' + path;
    
    unsigned int textureID;
    glGenTextures(1, &textureID);
    
    int width, height, nrChannels;
    unsigned char* data = stbi_load(filename.c_str(), &width, &height, 
                                   &nrChannels, 0);
    
    if (data) {
        GLenum format = GL_RGB;
        if (nrChannels == 1) format = GL_RED;
        else if (nrChannels == 3) format = GL_RGB;
        else if (nrChannels == 4) format = GL_RGBA;
        
        glBindTexture(GL_TEXTURE_2D, textureID);
        glTexImage2D(GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, format, width, height, 0,
                     format, GL_UNSIGNED_BYTE, data);
        glGenerateMipmap(GL_TEXTURE_2D);
        
        glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_WRAP_S, GL_REPEAT);
        glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_WRAP_T, GL_REPEAT);
        glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_MIN_FILTER, 
                       GL_LINEAR_MIPMAP_LINEAR);
        glTexParameteri(GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_MAG_FILTER, GL_LINEAR);
        
        stbi_image_free(data);
    } else {
        std::cerr << "Texture failed to load at path: " << filename << '\n';
        stbi_image_free(data);
    }
    
    return textureID;
}
```

---

# Using the Model Class

## Complete Example

```cpp
#include "Model.h"
#include "Shader.h"
#include "Camera.h"

int main() {
    // SDL and OpenGL initialization...
    
    Shader shader("shaders/model.vs", "shaders/model.fs");
    Model ourModel("models/nanosuit/nanosuit.obj");
    
    Camera camera(glm::vec3(0.0f, 0.0f, 3.0f));
    
    while (running) {
        // Handle events and camera input...
        
        glClear(GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT | GL_DEPTH_BUFFER_BIT);
        
        shader.use();
        
        // View/projection matrices
        glm::mat4 projection = glm::perspective(
            glm::radians(camera.getFov()),
            (float)SCREEN_WIDTH / SCREEN_HEIGHT,
            0.1f, 100.0f
        );
        glm::mat4 view = camera.getViewMatrix();
        shader.setMat4("projection", projection);
        shader.setMat4("view", view);
        
        // Model matrix
        glm::mat4 model = glm::mat4(1.0f);
        model = glm::translate(model, glm::vec3(0.0f, 0.0f, 0.0f));
        model = glm::scale(model, glm::vec3(1.0f));
        shader.setMat4("model", model);
        
        // Draw model
        ourModel.Draw(shader);
        
        SDL_GL_SwapWindow(window);
    }
    
    return 0;
}
```

## Vertex Shader for Models

```glsl
#version 330 core
layout (location = 0) in vec3 aPos;
layout (location = 1) in vec3 aNormal;
layout (location = 2) in vec2 aTexCoords;
layout (location = 3) in vec3 aTangent;
layout (location = 4) in vec3 aBitangent;

out vec3 FragPos;
out vec3 Normal;
out vec2 TexCoords;

uniform mat4 model;
uniform mat4 view;
uniform mat4 projection;

void main() {
    FragPos = vec3(model * vec4(aPos, 1.0));
    Normal = mat3(transpose(inverse(model))) * aNormal;
    TexCoords = aTexCoords;
    
    gl_Position = projection * view * vec4(FragPos, 1.0);
}
```

## Fragment Shader for Models

```glsl
#version 330 core
out vec4 FragColor;

in vec3 FragPos;
in vec3 Normal;
in vec2 TexCoords;

struct Material {
    sampler2D texture_diffuse1;
    sampler2D texture_specular1;
    float shininess;
};

uniform Material material;
uniform vec3 viewPos;
uniform vec3 lightPos;
uniform vec3 lightColor;

void main() {
    // Ambient
    vec3 ambient = 0.1 * lightColor * vec3(texture(material.texture_diffuse1, TexCoords));
    
    // Diffuse
    vec3 norm = normalize(Normal);
    vec3 lightDir = normalize(lightPos - FragPos);
    float diff = max(dot(norm, lightDir), 0.0);
    vec3 diffuse = diff * lightColor * vec3(texture(material.texture_diffuse1, TexCoords));
    
    // Specular
    vec3 viewDir = normalize(viewPos - FragPos);
    vec3 reflectDir = reflect(-lightDir, norm);
    float spec = pow(max(dot(viewDir, reflectDir), 0.0), material.shininess);
    vec3 specular = spec * lightColor * vec3(texture(material.texture_specular1, TexCoords));
    
    vec3 result = ambient + diffuse + specular;
    FragColor = vec4(result, 1.0);
}
```

---

# Advanced Topics

## Multiple Texture Types

```cpp
// In your shader:
uniform sampler2D texture_diffuse1;
uniform sampler2D texture_diffuse2;
uniform sampler2D texture_specular1;
uniform sampler2D texture_normal1;
uniform sampler2D texture_height1;
```

::: {.fragment}
The `Mesh::Draw` function automatically binds textures with appropriate names!
:::

## Model Transformations

```cpp
// Translate
model = glm::translate(model, glm::vec3(0.0f, -1.75f, 0.0f));

// Scale (uniform)
model = glm::scale(model, glm::vec3(0.2f));

// Rotate
model = glm::rotate(model, glm::radians(angle), glm::vec3(0.0f, 1.0f, 0.0f));

// Combine
model = glm::translate(glm::mat4(1.0f), position);
model = glm::rotate(model, glm::radians(angle), axis);
model = glm::scale(model, scale);
```

## Performance Considerations

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Optimization Tips:**

- Load models once, reuse
- Use instancing for many copies
- Combine meshes when possible
- Use level of detail (LOD)
- Frustum culling
- Occlusion culling
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Memory Management:**

- Cache loaded textures
- Delete models when not needed
- Use texture atlases
- Compress textures
- Consider streaming for large scenes
:::
:::

## Handling Large Models

```cpp
class ModelManager {
    std::unordered_map<std::string, std::shared_ptr<Model>> models;
    
public:
    std::shared_ptr<Model> getModel(const std::string& path) {
        auto it = models.find(path);
        if (it != models.end()) {
            return it->second;
        }
        
        auto model = std::make_shared<Model>(path);
        models[path] = model;
        return model;
    }
};
```

::: {.callout-tip}
**Resource management**: Cache models to avoid loading same model multiple times!
:::

---

# Common Issues and Solutions

## Troubleshooting

::: {.panel-tabset}
### Model Not Loading
```cpp
// Check file path
if (!std::filesystem::exists(path)) {
    std::cerr << "Model file not found: " << path << '\n';
}

// Check Assimp errors
if (!scene) {
    std::cerr << "Assimp error: " << importer.GetErrorString() << '\n';
}
```

### Wrong Orientation
```cpp
// Try different rotations
model = glm::rotate(model, glm::radians(180.0f), glm::vec3(1, 0, 0));

// Or fix in Blender before exporting
```

### Textures Not Loading
```cpp
// Check texture paths (relative to model file)
std::string texPath = directory + '/' + str.C_Str();
std::cout << "Loading texture: " << texPath << '\n';

// Verify texture file exists
```

### Performance Issues
```cpp
// Enable optimization flags
aiProcess_OptimizeMeshes | 
aiProcess_OptimizeGraph |
aiProcess_JoinIdenticalVertices

// Consider LOD system
```
:::

## Model Scale Issues

```cpp
// If model is too large or small, scale it
glm::mat4 model = glm::mat4(1.0f);

// For models exported from Blender (often in cm)
model = glm::scale(model, glm::vec3(0.01f));

// Or adjust in modeling software before export
```

---

# Best Practices

## Model Pipeline

::: {.incremental}
1. **Create** model in Blender/Maya
2. **UV unwrap** for proper texturing
3. **Apply transformations** (scale, rotation)
4. **Export** in appropriate format (GLTF recommended)
5. **Test** in model viewer first
6. **Optimize** polycount if needed
7. **Load** in your application
:::

## Recommended Export Settings

**Blender → GLTF:**

- Apply modifiers
- Apply transformations
- Y up, Z forward
- Include normals and tangents
- Embed textures or use relative paths

**Blender → OBJ:**

- Triangulate faces
- Include normals
- Include UVs
- Forward: -Z, Up: Y

## Project Organization

```
project/
├── src/
│   ├── main.cpp
│   ├── Model.h
│   ├── Model.cpp
│   ├── Mesh.h
│   └── Mesh.cpp
├── shaders/
│   ├── model.vs
│   └── model.fs
├── models/
│   ├── character/
│   │   ├── character.gltf
│   │   └── textures/
│   ├── environment/
│   └── props/
└── textures/
    └── common/
```

---

# What We've Learned

## Key Concepts

::: {.incremental}
- **Model file formats** and their differences
- **Assimp** library for unified model loading
- **Scene graph** and hierarchical transformations
- **Mesh class** for efficient rendering
- **Model class** for managing multiple meshes
- **Material and texture** handling
- **Post-processing** for optimization
- **Resource management** and caching
:::

## Next Steps

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Practice:**

- Load your own OBJ/GLTF models
- Experiment with transformations
- Add multiple models to scene
- Implement model picking
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Advanced:**

- Skeletal animation
- Morph targets/blend shapes
- Level of detail (LOD)
- Model instancing
- Scene management
:::
:::

## Resources

**Tools:**

- [Blender](https://www.blender.org/) - Free 3D modeling software
- [Assimp](https://www.assimp.org/) - Asset importer library
- [cgtrader.com](https://www.cgtrader.com/) - Free/paid models
- [sketchfab.com](https://sketchfab.com/) - 3D model repository

**Learning:**

- [Learn OpenGL - Model Loading](https://learnopengl.com/Model-Loading/Assimp)
- Assimp documentation
- GLTF 2.0 specification

---

# Questions?

::: {.r-fit-text}
Thank you!
:::

::: {.fragment}
Loading 3D models opens up endless possibilities for creating rich, detailed 3D scenes!
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Next**: We'll explore advanced lighting techniques and shadows! 🎨
:::
